JEREMY CLOYD, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF VIA COURTCALL. TERRENCE BRASCH, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS VIA COURTCALL. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT DID NOT FIND GOOD CAUSE TO CONTINUE TRIAL BASED ON MR. BRASCH TRIAL CONFLICT BUT FINDS THAT GOOD CAUSE EXISTS UNDER CRC 3.1332(C)(5) TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE COURT VACATES THE TRIAL DATE OF 9/21/20 AND A TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 3/22/21 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ALL PRETRIAL DEADLINES SHALL GOVERN THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MR. BRASCH TO SERVE THE ORDER. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-15-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF KEVIN RONAN's Motion To Compel Discovery; Request For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion of Kevin Ronan is GRANTED as follows:

1) Requests for Production (Set One)

The objection by Big Dog City Corporation ("Big Dog") to Request No. 20 is overruled. Big Dog's modest right of privacy as a business entity is outweighed by the relevance of the information sought to the subject matter in the pending action. "Doubts as to relevance should generally be resolved in favor of permitting discovery." SCC Acquisitions, Inc. v. Superior Court (2015) 243 Cal. App. 4th 741, 755-56. This finding is without prejudice to Golden Sand's ability to seek a protective order preventing the disclosure of the requested information.

The court directs Big Dog to serve verified code-compliant further responses to Requests Nos. 6, 7, 10-12 & 20 by February 5, 2021 and produce all responsive non-privileged documents within its possession, custody or control by February 15, 2021. If it withholds documents on grounds of privilege or privacy, it shall provide a log concurrent with its document production, including the specific grounds of each claim of privilege or privacy.

The term "code-compliant" means in compliance with the California Civil Discovery Act, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2016.010 et seq., as interpreted by the decisions of the courts of this state, including, without limitation, the case decisions cited in Mr. Ronan's moving papers.

2) Special Interrogatories (Set One)

Big Dog's objections to Special Interrogatories Nos. 4 & 13 are overruled for the same reason as Request for Production No. 20, and this finding is likewise without prejudice to Golden Sand's ability to seek a protective order preventing the disclosure of the requested information. Big Dog shall serve verified code-compliant further responses to Special Interrogatories Nos. 4 & 13 by February 5, 2021.

3) Sanctions

Big Dog's counsel of record, Aaron Hancock and Terrence Brasch, are further ordered to pay $3,000 in monetary sanctions to Mr. Ronan if Big Dog does not timely comply with this order.

The Court has received a proposed order from the prevailing party which it will review.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, To Deem Admissions, And To Grant Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeremy Cloyd, Esq. of Altair Law T:415.988.9828, for plaintiff and moving party;
Terrence J. Brasch, Esq. of Bishop Barry, T:510.596.0888, for defendant and opposing party.

Both parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: Hearing held on PLAINTIFF KEVIN RONAN's Motion To Compel Discovery, To Deem Admissions, And To Grant Monetary Sanctions. After hearing, Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein ruled as follows:

Plaintiff's motion is denied as moot. Defendant responded to all outstanding discovery prior to the hearing. Pursuant to CCP 2033.280(C) sanctions are awarded to the plaintiff in the sum of $810. Court to prepare an order.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
